text.title: Interview Note Synthesis (AI-Assisted) Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Interview Note Synthesis (AI-Assisted) application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Extract verbatim quotes with tags BEFORE attempting cluster synthesis. AI clusters on noise if asked to extract + cluster in one pass.
rule.rationale: Two-stage pipeline (extract → cluster) reliably outperforms single-stage; quality 20-40% better in head-to-head tests.

rule.statement: AI proposes clusters; human reviews + renames + merges. Pure-AI clusters look plausible but mislabel boundary cases at 20-40% rate.
rule.rationale: AI clustering on small samples is overconfident; human review is the calibration step.

rule.statement: A theme is 'signal' if mentioned by ≥3 distinct interviewees AND ≥2 are recent (≤90 days). One enthusiastic interviewee ≠ signal.
rule.rationale: Anecdotes feel powerful and bias OST; explicit threshold prevents single-voice over-weighting.

rule.statement: Synthesis output explicitly feeds OST: opportunities phrased as customer outcomes, evidence linked, priority score attached.
rule.rationale: Without OST-shape, synthesis is a doc; with OST-shape, it's an artefact downstream can use.

rule.statement: Every claim cites ≥2 verbatim quotes with transcript IDs. Paraphrased claims are dropped.
rule.rationale: Verbatim citation is the falsifiability test; paraphrase is where hallucination enters.
